P513098.Description: What it is: A daily leave-on serum, formulated with hyaluronic acid, that offers intense hydration to soothe dry scalps and support long-term scalp health. Ingredient Callouts: This product is vegan, gluten-free, and cruelty-free. What Else You Need to Know: Skincare extends to your scalp. Hydrate it for strong-, healthy-looking hair. Daily Hydro? Serum, with hyaluronic acid, delivers hydration to both scalp and hair, while amino acids lock in moisture and support the scalp barrier. Fragrance Description: Fragrance free Clean at Sephora

P513098.ReviewText: I’ve been using the Act+Acre Daily Hydro Scalp Serum for a few weeks now, and I can honestly say it’s a game-changer in my scalp care routine. My scalp tends to get dry and itchy—especially after wash days or in colder weather—and this serum brought instant relief without leaving any greasy residue. The texture is super lightweight, almost like water, and absorbs quickly. I love that it’s fragrance-free, so it doesn’t compete with my other products or irritate my skin. It gives a nice cooling sensation when applied, which feels soothing and refreshing. I use it daily (sometimes twice if my scalp feels extra dry), and I’ve noticed way less flaking and tightness. My scalp feels healthier and more balanced, and I even feel like it’s helping with overall hair growth since my roots seem fuller. Complimentary from Act + Acre. It’s a bit of a splurge, but if you’re serious about scalp health or dealing with dryness, it’s 100% worth it.

The lightweight formula sinks in beautifully, making it perfect for both morning and night. I especially love how it gives me that healthy, dewy glow while keeping my skin balanced all day long. Plus, knowing it’s packed with clean, nourishing ingredients makes me feel even better about using it daily. If you’re looking for a hydrating hero that actually delivers, this is it! P513098.OriginalProductName: Daily Hydro? Scalp Serum with Hyaluronic Acid for Dry Scalp
P513098.ReviewText: Gifted by Act + Acre. The Act + Acre Hydro Serum is a brand new addition to my routine, especially with how dry my hair and scalp have been after years of coloring. It’s lightweight, absorbs quickly, and doesn’t leave any greasy residue. The multi-molecular hyaluronic acid definitely helps with hydration—I notice less flaking and irritation when I use it consistently. The amino acids and glycerin also seem to help with scalp health, though I can’t say I’ve seen a major difference in hair strength just yet. I apply it to my damp scalp after washing, massage it in, and then put my hair in a loose braid. When I take it out, I get soft, natural waves without feeling like there’s any product buildup. The fact that it’s clean, vegan, and free of harsh ingredients is a plus. While it’s not an instant miracle fix, it does make my scalp feel healthier and my hair look more hydrated over time. If you struggle with dryness or irritation, it’s worth trying—but it works best when you use it consistently.
P513098.Title: I love the Act + Acre brand, great new addition!!

P513880.Description: What it is: A pH-optimized leave-on scalp serum designed to extend time between washes and control excess oil and odor. Hair Texture: Straight, Wavy, Curly, and Coily Hair Type: Fine, Medium, and Thick Hair Concerns: - Color Safe - Oily Scalp Key Benefits: - Balances scalp pH to prevent excess oil production - Extends time between washes - Stabilizes the scalp to be less oily over time Highlighted Ingredients: - 4% Niacinamide: A topical vitamin B3 that balances oil levels. - 5% Hyper-Fermented Green Tea Blend: Molecular antioxidant targets excess oil. - 2% Bio-Fermented Astragalus: Supports collagen and hyaluronic acid production for moisturized skin. Ingredient Callouts: This product is vegan, gluten-free, cruelty-free, and comes in recyclable packaging. What Else You Need to Know: Niacinamide is a water-soluble form of vitamin B3. Niacinamide is an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory ingredient to support regulated oil production. Act+Acre uses bio- and hyper-fermented ingredients in this formula to create a multitude of smaller molecules that can better penetrate the scalp for boosted efficacy and pH optimization of the scalp microbiome. Fragrance Description: Fragrance-free. Clean at Sephora is our commitment to offering effective products without certain ingredients, such as parabens, sulfates, phthalates, and mineral oils. Learn more here.

This is my first time ever experiencing this brand and a product of this nature for the scalp to control oil production. As a girl that usually has to wash my hair every other day because otherwise it will feel too uncomfortable with the buildup, here is what I have to say:
I’m very impressed with how much it’s helped with my oily scalp. Before this, my hair would look greasy around the roots on the second day but after using this serum consistently, I’ve noticed a big difference not just in how my hair and scalp feels, but how it looks too! It stays fresher for longer, which means I can now go longer without having to wash my hair without feeling like my hair looks dirty, itchy or flat, especially around my bangs. What I really like is that it doesn’t feel like it’s just covering up oiliness; it actually seems to be reducing how much oil my scalp produces over time.
I want to mention that it also helps during shower time too, as I usually have to shampoo two to three times to actually feel clean, while using this product, it has definitely reduced the amount of times needed and creating a rich lather on the get go is just a breezy now! This is definitely a keeper for me!

Skin Type: Normal and Dry Skincare Concerns: Dryness Formulation: Cream Highlighted Ingredients: - Patented Barrier-Boosting Ceramides Blend: High-density ceramides fill moisture gaps in sensitive skin for hydration. - Capsule Technology: Keeps ceramides in the skin barrier longer for hydration. - Triple Lipid Complex: Ceramides, fatty acids, and cholesterol mimic lipids to moisturize. Ingredient Callouts: Free of parabens, formaldehydes, formaldehyde-releasing agents, phthalates, mineral oil, retinyl palmitate, oxybenzone, coal tar, hydroquinone, sulfates SLS & SLES, triclocarban, triclosan, and contains less than one percent synthetic fragrance. It is also vegan and gluten-free. What Else You Need to Know: AESTURA is the #1 derm-recommended brand in Korea for sensitive skin. The cream contains barrier-boosting ceramides that are delivered to skin through unique capsule technology. It is suitable for use after microneedling treatments and reduces hydration loss, visible redness and roughness post-microneedling. ATOBARRIER365 Cream is non-comedogenic, fragrance-free, alcohol-free, dermatologist-tested. It is recognized by the National Eczema Association with the Seal of Acceptance. Clinical Results: In an instrumental result, 32 women, after one application: - Provides 120 hours of hydration - Strengthens barrier by +39.2% - Improves flakiness by +39.7% - 100% showed improvement in skin barrier In a self-scoring, four-week clinical study on 22 women, after four weeks: - Skin sensitivity reduced by 51% Result after repeated tape-stripping, baseline vs. four weeks, on 32 women: - Skin's natural ability to defend itself from damage improved by 38% In a self-scoring clinical study on 32 women, after four weeks: - Subjects felt +132% improvement in hydration and +152% improvement in dryness In a self-assessment on 32 women, after four weeks of daily use: - 96% agree skin barrier feels protected - 96% agree skin has a healthy glow - 93% agree skin barrier feels healthier

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText:
Usually I love to try new things, but I’m on my second purchase of this and it’s definitely the new staple in my routine! I have combination skin, that runs a little more dry, but this keeps me moisturized & glowy all day long. I’m breakout prone and have sensitive skin, but this is one of the only products I’ve had no issues with. It goes on very thick, so I only need about a pea sized amount. This whole bottle lasts me ~60 days, which I was so pleased with. 

If you’re in a warmer climate, it can feel a little greasy when humid, but that’d be my only complaint.
P515494.Title: My favorite moisturizer EVER!

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText:
I have used this product for years and purchase it exclusively from reputable Korean retailers. As a 51-year-old skincare enthusiast who performs biweekly chemical peels, my skin is extremely reactive and sensitive — yet this line has never caused irritation or breakouts for me.

I believe part of the misunderstanding around this brand comes from how heavily we rely on active ingredients in the U.S. While actives can be beneficial, overuse often leads to dehydration, dullness, and a compromised barrier. What makes this line exceptional is its focus on restoring the skin’s foundational lipids — particularly cholesterol and fatty acids — which are essential for rebuilding the barrier, improving texture, softening the look of pores, and restoring plumpness.

The light blue version is ideal for daytime use due to its lighter texture, while the white version provides deeper overnight support. I consistently wake up with calm, balanced, resilient skin.

This brand is widely respected in Korea for its use of MLE (Multi-Lamellar Emulsion) technology, which mimics the skin’s natural lipid structure and helps reinforce barrier integrity at a structural level. That formulation approach is what truly sets it apart.

In my experience, few products available in the U.S. market compare in terms of barrier support and long-term skin health.
P515494.Title: A must read review. A MATURE skin must have

P515494.ReviewText: If you prefer moisturizers that soak in completely and feel like nothing is on your skin, this is not the moisturizer for you (try a tub of the First Aid Beauty Ultra repair cream instead). For myself, I NEED a moisturizer that leaves an occlusive layer or my skin starts to get flaky. That being said, this one doesn't feel greasy or heavy, it just also doesn't feel like nothing is there. I use a foundation with more of a serum consistency (Dior Backstage Face & Body) and have zero issues with it breaking up over this moisturizer. I'm not sure how a foundation with a more matte and full coverage finish would fare, but it's definitely worth a try if you have very dry or dehydrated skin.

No itch. No flushing. No waking up a bit greasy. Just pure hydration and cool, calm skin. I recently added azelaic acid to my routine and the combo have saved my face. It's the dead of a brutal winter right now but I'm glowing. I might switch to the lotion version (this is the cream) for summer. P515494.Title: The best I've ever tried in all my years

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText:
I have sensitive, rosacea-prone skin that gets dry and reactive easily, so I’m very careful about what I put on my face. I normally rotate between high-end brands like Estée Lauder and everyday staples like CeraVe, but the Aestura Ato Barrier 365 Cream has become a favorite because it’s hydrating, soothing, and doesn’t cause breakouts—which is usually my main concern with richer creams.

The texture is thick and luxurious but absorbs beautifully, leaving my skin soft and comfortable without any greasy residue. I started with the Aestura Hydrogel, which was perfect for humid Hawaii, but when I traveled to Las Vegas, this cream was the only one that kept my skin hydrated all day without re-application or mists. I continue to use it back in Hawaii during cooler, drier days, and it works wonderfully there too.

Over time, my skin barrier feels stronger, my redness is calmer, and my overall skin texture is smoother. This cream proves that affordable skincare can truly deliver results, and it’s now a staple in my daily routine. Highly recommend for anyone with sensitive or reactive skin who wants effective, reliable hydration.
P515494.Title: Barrier Keeper. Glow Deeper.

My skin has been breaking out recently and this moisturizer is so soothing, whether I wear it during the day or in the night. It gives you that glassy skin that people talk about without being heavy or oily or greasy. I do see how people mention the silicone but honestly, I feel like my skin absorbs it soon after I apply it, probably like an hour within applying (not to the point where I feel like there is no reason to apply it, but like it truly is nourishing for my skin.) I tend to not buy new products because I have things that work fine and again I tend to only buy luxury skin care when it’s at Costco and on sale. But I have a feeling that will definitely be repurchasing this!

P515494.ReviewText: One of the Sephora employees recommended me to pair this with the Paula's Choice Azelaic Acid that I originally came in for, saying they work great together and that it has helped her eczema. She gave me samples to last a couple weeks to really see if I'd like it and I loved it so went back to purchase! I can't say I've tracked whether it brings down my limited facial redness from rosacea, as it's mostly on my chest, but I think it can make subtle difference. I wake up with soft skin when I use this regularly. To illustrate how much I love this thing -- I am proud of them for making it to Sephora. 

Who knows, this could be your "one," too. It's definitely worth trying. No hate panning here.

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText: I have dry and dehydrated skin. I started tretinoin a while ago. At the time, I was using the Avene TriXera Nutrition balm until it got discontinued. I wanted to try the Aestura cream because it has ceramides and cholesterol similar to the Avene. My skin was peeling and burning as expected from tretinoin. When I used the Aestura cream, it calmed down the burning sensation and smoothed the peeling skin. A year later, my skin has adapted to tretinoin without any issues. I use tretinoin 3 times a week and I use the Aestura cream every night. I wake up with moisturized skin in the morning. I don't use this product in the morning because it's a bit heavy under my sunscreen. I use the Cosrx hyaluronic acid cream instead because it's super lightweight. The only issue I have with this product is that the spreadability is not that great. Because I have dry skin, I apply moisturizers when my face is still a little bit wet or damp, and I find that this product doesn't spread nicely on top of wet skin. Other than that, it's a fantastic product and I will continue to buy it. One bottle lasted me 5 months! It's pricey, but for me it's worth it.
P515494.Title: Perfect with tretinoin

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText: I love korean beauty and im glad it's finally making it's way here to the US. This cream is exactly what i expected it to be and more. I live in a colder state where it's been negatives recently and my skin has been so dry. With this cream my skin has felt so hydrated and nourished. My skin loved it so much and it did not break me out. It also worked well with my other skincare and makeup

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText: The ATOBARRIER365 Cream with Ceramides is hydrating and I love the visible ceramides. I was curious about the differences between this brand's products as they are all labelled for hydration/moisturization and the skin barrier. They all hydrate my dry skin. I believe the soothing cream is best for the skin barrier. The lotion has a similar texture when applying but maybe slightly lighter. The regular cream contains visible ceramide capsules that burst open when applied to the skin. I feel that only one or two of these products are necessary. I would apply the cream first, followed by the soothing cream, as it is thicker and helps to lock in the ceramides.

P515494.OriginalProductName: Mini ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText:
Got a damaged skin barrier from tret or over-exfoliation? Wind burn? Sad sensitive skin in general? My friend, this is THE CREAM FOR YOU. 

This has been my holy grail barrier repair cream for years. Seriously desert-island, “if I could only own a single skincare product for the rest of my life” stuff. I’ve been purchasing it on Korean import sites, and I’m so excited that more people are getting easy access to it now. I plan to switch to Sephora when I run out of my current tube since the ingredients are identical.

I’ve got the sort of face that gets irritated if you look at it wrong: rosacea, hormonal acne, extreme fragrance sensitivities, apparent inability to hold onto moisture, the works. Nothing has been better for me than this at night and in the winter, with the corresponding Hydro cream (blue tube, a little lighter in texture) in the morning and during humid summer weather. 

If you’re like me, you’ll understand when I say this stuff is boring in the best way. It’s absolutely packed with soothing and moisturizing ingredients that have years of science behind them. It applies easily and absorbs surprisingly quickly for something so dense. It’s amazing. I love it. I hope you’ll love it too.
P515494.Title: The gold standard in barrier repair!

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText: I absolutely love this cream. It’s my go to whenever I need an extra layer of protection and moisture. I highly recommend for all that get facial treatments during winter as this has helped my skin from purging.  It leaves my skin glowing!

P515494.ReviewText: Sephora carrying this brand is a blessing. I have always used this cream, it works so well. I use it with my tret and it does not give me flakes in the mornjng. If has ceramides in it which plump your skin and keep it hydrated. So easy to shop at sephora for it now.

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText:
I used to buy this abroad or shipped from Korea. The formula change of removing high grade silicones and waxes in exchange for Niacinamide just turns this to another ceramide moisturizer. Might as well buy CeraVa, Vanicream or Prequel you know? The little balls are just gimmicky now. Plus now it’s less occlusive. In plain English, it doesn’t lock in moisture and hydration as much anymore. 

And Niacinamide is an excellent ingredient for brightening (any dermatologist would agree), but it’s a no-no for the usual target market (former I guess?) of this product who have reallly realllly sensitive, irritated skin who wants BARRIER repair with no actives. Some might still be okay with the Niacinamide, but think post tretinoin, eczema or rosacea.

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText:
Disclaimers: 
- I never used the old version of this, and I only used this product twice. 
- My skin is very sensitive/reactive. 
- My normal moisturizer is Cetaphil moisturizing cream, and my comparisons use this as a control.

I heard good things about this moisturizer, and wanted to try something different, and of course I have been wanting to steer more towards Korean skincare as its gentler and often times more affordable. However, after two uses:
- I noticed that this cream is not as moisturizing as my Cetaphil cream, I could feel some tightness in my skin, even after applying another layer. 
- It didn't make me feel greasy or look oily, however, it did feel like it was getting into my eyes and making them feel filmy, if you know what I mean. 
- It didn't irritate my skin or make it sore/itchy, however I did get a few small whiteheads on my cheeks and forehead, and also developed a pimple under my nostril. I'm not super acne prone, so this was a red flag for me. 
- And for those who are sensitive to smells, it is unscented, but it does have that unscented scent which was also a little off for me.

Is it horrible? No. It will work well for many people. Did it work for me? Unfortunately, no.

P515494.ReviewText:
This product doesn't work for me.  I experienced breakouts that worsened with continued use and stopped with discontinuing the product.  Additionally, I didn't feel it worked well under my makeup no matter which products I used or how I tried to make it work. My foundation was always either slipping off and smearing on everything which I never have an issue with, or I was constantly powdering my face because it felt super greasy which made my makeup feel heavy. I just couldn't find a way to make it work for me. 
The product feels nice and goes on the skin lovely and if it didn't cause breakouts I would continue for night and days I don't wear make-up.  I do have super sensitive skin, and it didn't cause any irritation which is why I tried so hard to make it work.

P515494.ReviewText: I’ve been using this cream for years, and I was so happy Sephora finally started selling it, as it would save me time from ordering it on other sites. However, I was disappointed when I opened it and noticed the difference immediately. The product on the left is the original, and on the right is the one I received from sephora. The texture of the original is more of a thick creamy consistency, and the one I received is more sticky and sunscreen consistency. It feels completely different on the face as well. I have sensitive skin, and it doesn’t feel like it moisturizes the same anymore. I’m not sure if they changed the formula, or rebranded but this is not the product I’ve been using. I have to return this.

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText: The product feels nice but broke me out. I wasn’t sure at first so I stopped using the product and went back to my usual (and I use topical acne medication). After my skin cleared up I decided to try this cream again but it caused another set of breaking.

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText: At the beginning, I kind of liked this product especially because it spreads so well. However, the more I used it, the more I realized of two things: 1) my skin started to feel more dehydrated than usual (I have oily dehydrated skin) and my eyes would get so irritated in the evening and in the morning they would burn. No cream has ever caused me this no matter how close I get to the eye area. This cream is greasy but I didn’t care because I used it at night, however, it wasn’t effective at all and because it is not hydrating, my barrier feels worse. It’s great for the hands though.
P515494.Title: Not hydrating but greasy and irritating

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText: I’ve been using this for almost two months, and I wish I was more impressed. The texture is beautiful, but it takes so long to absorb into my skin. There’s no stickiness, but just feeling it sit on my face for what feels like 17 light years is very annoying. It’s a guessing game each time trying not to apply way too much or way too little. Also, I haven’t found it to be moisturizing. If anything, the texture of my skin has gotten worse. The cold weather where I live could be a contributing factor, but I’m still disappointed that such a nice feeling cream left my skin congested and dehydrated (and sometimes a little flaky). If you’re sensitive or acne-prone, I’d either give this a patch test or skip it unfortunately.

P515494.Title: Almost perfect - but drying

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText:
This moisturizer is deeply hydrating. My skin feels so plump and comfortable when I use it, especially at night. It gives that rich, cocooned feeling without actually clogging my pores or breaking me out.

The texture is interesting — it feels oily going on, but it’s not greasy in a heavy or pore-clogging way. It kind of melts into the skin and creates a protective barrier, which I love for nighttime.

For my dry girlies: I am super, super dry, and this is incredibly hydrating at night. I wish I could use it in the morning too because my skin drinks it up. But alas… I cannot. The slight oily slip makes it tricky under makeup and doesn’t sit the best under foundation, so it’s not my favorite for daytime wear.

Overall, it’s an amazing nighttime moisturizer for dry or barrier-compromised skin. Just not my first choice for daytime under makeup. 4/5!
P515494.Title: Great night time moisturizer!

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText: I only recently learned that a true barrier cream includes three specific ingredients. This has totally saved my skin this winter. It’s so soothing and protective. Will definitely repurchase.

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText: This barrier cream has been a lifesaver for my insanely dry winter skin. At night, I slather it on as the last step in my routine, and by morning, my skin still feels soft and hydrated. In the morning, I use a light layer before applying my tinted serum, and it keeps my makeup locked in all day without any dry patches or flaking. The texture is rich but not heavy, and it actually feels like it’s sealing in moisture instead of just sitting on top of my skin. Between this and drinking tons of water, my skin is finally coming back to life after months of being dull and tight. The ceramides and lipids really make a difference—I don’t get that midday dehydration where my skin feels like it’s pulling. I also love that it’s dermatologist-tested and designed to strengthen the skin barrier over time. It’s not just a quick fix; it actually improves dryness and flakiness with continued use. If your skin gets painfully dry in the winter like mine, this is worth trying.

P515494.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515494.ReviewText: Atobarrier 365 Cream with Ceramide is a standout product for anyone seeking deep hydration and skin barrier repair. This rich, yet lightweight moisturizer is formulated with ceramides, which are key for strengthening the skin’s natural barrier and locking in moisture. Perfect for dry, sensitive, or even compromised skin, it provides an immediate soothing effect while helping to restore the skin’s lipid barrier over time. The texture is silky, absorbing quickly without leaving any greasy residue, which makes it a great choice for both daytime and nighttime use. After a few days of consistent use, you’ll notice your skin feels more supple and resilient, with a noticeable reduction in dryness or irritation. It’s especially beneficial for people with conditions like eczema or those living in harsh climates where the skin is constantly exposed to environmental stressors. The fragrance-free formula is a bonus for sensitive skin, and it’s suitable for all skin types, even those prone to allergies. Pros: • Deeply hydrates and nourishes skin • Contains ceramides to restore the skin barrier • Lightweight, fast-absorbing formula • Fragrance-free, suitable for sensitive skin • Great for dry or irritated skin, including eczema Cons: • May not be rich enough for very dry skin types in extreme conditions • On the pricier side for a drugstore moisturizer Overall, Atobarrier 365 is a reliable, gentle cream that lives up to its claims of providing long-lasting moisture and skin barrier repair. It’s a solid choice for daily use, especially if you need something that works without causing irritation.

P515491.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Hydro CERA-HA Ceramide + Hyaluronic Acid Face Serum for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515491.ReviewText: Atobarrier 365 Hydro Cera-HA Serum is a game-changer for anyone looking to deeply hydrate and strengthen their skin. This serum blends the power of ceramides and hyaluronic acid, two key ingredients that work in harmony to boost hydration and repair the skin’s protective barrier. Ceramides help restore the skin’s natural barrier function, while hyaluronic acid draws moisture deep into the skin for long-lasting hydration. The texture of the serum is lightweight and slightly gel-like, making it perfect for layering under other products. It absorbs quickly without leaving any stickiness or residue, which makes it ideal for both morning and nighttime routines. After consistent use, you’ll notice that your skin feels smoother, plumper, and more resilient. It also does a great job at calming dry or irritated areas, making it a good option for sensitive skin. One of the standout features is its ability to provide intense hydration without feeling heavy or greasy. If you live in a dry climate or struggle with dehydrated skin, this serum can be a game-changer. Plus, the formula is free from fragrances and irritating additives, which is great for those with sensitive or reactive skin. Pros: • Deeply hydrates and plumps skin with hyaluronic acid • Restores skin barrier with ceramides • Lightweight, non-greasy formula that absorbs quickly • Calms and soothes sensitive or dry skin • Fragrance-free, ideal for sensitive skin Cons: • May need additional moisturizing for very dry skin in extreme conditions • Slightly higher price point than other serums in the same category Overall, the Atobarrier 365 Hydro Cera-HA Serum is an excellent addition to any skincare routine, especially for those seeking deep hydration and barrier repair. Its gentle yet effective formula is perfect for those with sensitive, dry, or irritated skin. It delivers visible results in terms of smoother, more hydrated skin without overwhelming the complexion.
P515491.Title: Amazing to have

P515486.ReviewText:
Usually I love to try new things, but I’m on my second purchase of this and it’s definitely the new staple in my routine! I have combination skin, that runs a little more dry, but this keeps me moisturized & glowy all day long. I’m breakout prone and have sensitive skin, but this is one of the only products I’ve had no issues with. It goes on very thick, so I only need about a pea sized amount. This whole bottle lasts me ~60 days, which I was so pleased with. 

If you’re in a warmer climate, it can feel a little greasy when humid, but that’d be my only complaint.
P515486.Title: My favorite moisturizer EVER!

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText:
I have used this product for years and purchase it exclusively from reputable Korean retailers. As a 51-year-old skincare enthusiast who performs biweekly chemical peels, my skin is extremely reactive and sensitive — yet this line has never caused irritation or breakouts for me.

I believe part of the misunderstanding around this brand comes from how heavily we rely on active ingredients in the U.S. While actives can be beneficial, overuse often leads to dehydration, dullness, and a compromised barrier. What makes this line exceptional is its focus on restoring the skin’s foundational lipids — particularly cholesterol and fatty acids — which are essential for rebuilding the barrier, improving texture, softening the look of pores, and restoring plumpness.

The light blue version is ideal for daytime use due to its lighter texture, while the white version provides deeper overnight support. I consistently wake up with calm, balanced, resilient skin.

This brand is widely respected in Korea for its use of MLE (Multi-Lamellar Emulsion) technology, which mimics the skin’s natural lipid structure and helps reinforce barrier integrity at a structural level. That formulation approach is what truly sets it apart.

In my experience, few products available in the U.S. market compare in terms of barrier support and long-term skin health.
P515486.Title: A must read review. A MATURE skin must have

It's the dead of a brutal winter right now but I'm glowing. I might switch to the lotion version (this is the cream) for summer. I'm going to explore the rest of this brand and try not to ruin my good luck!
P515486.Title: The best I've ever tried in all my years

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText:
I have sensitive, rosacea-prone skin that gets dry and reactive easily, so I’m very careful about what I put on my face. I normally rotate between high-end brands like Estée Lauder and everyday staples like CeraVe, but the Aestura Ato Barrier 365 Cream has become a favorite because it’s hydrating, soothing, and doesn’t cause breakouts—which is usually my main concern with richer creams.

The texture is thick and luxurious but absorbs beautifully, leaving my skin soft and comfortable without any greasy residue. I started with the Aestura Hydrogel, which was perfect for humid Hawaii, but when I traveled to Las Vegas, this cream was the only one that kept my skin hydrated all day without re-application or mists. I continue to use it back in Hawaii during cooler, drier days, and it works wonderfully there too.

Over time, my skin barrier feels stronger, my redness is calmer, and my overall skin texture is smoother. This cream proves that affordable skincare can truly deliver results, and it’s now a staple in my daily routine. Highly recommend for anyone with sensitive or reactive skin who wants effective, reliable hydration.
P515486.Title: Barrier Keeper. Glow Deeper.

P515486.ReviewText: I put this item on my Christmas wish list, as I heard about this product from a beauty influencer, but didn’t wanna spend the money on it myself. My skin has been breaking out recently and this moisturizer is so soothing, whether I wear it during the day or in the night. It gives you that glassy skin that people talk about without being heavy or oily or greasy. I do see how people mention the silicone but honestly, I feel like my skin absorbs it soon after I apply it, probably like an hour within applying (not to the point where I feel like there is no reason to apply it, but like it truly is nourishing for my skin.) I tend to not buy new products because I have things that work fine and again I tend to only buy luxury skin care when it’s at Costco and on sale. But I have a feeling that will definitely be repurchasing this!

It gives a very lit from within glow without feeling greasy or heavy. My skin soaks this up and is left feeling comfortable and very natural. Layers beautifully under sunscreen and makeup. It’s gentle enough that I can use this after sunburns or if I’ve experienced a reaction from another product. It is sooo soothing, like I notice a visible reduction in redness ten minutes after I apply it. I use it both morning and night and I’ve paired it with their foaming cleanser which is also amazing and so gentle. My skin has cleared up almost completely since using this and I feel like I have my normal, healthy skin back. Wish I could give it ten stars. <3

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText:
Every skincare lover is on a journey--a quest, if you will--to find their moisturizer soulmate. When I found this randomly on Amazon, that was it for me--I found "the one." 

It feels like one part gel texture, one part rich cream. In my experience of 2+ years, it is safe for acne and rosacea and feels fairly soothing (though I go for heavier creams when I need really soothing). I can't say I've tracked whether it brings down my limited facial redness from rosacea, as it's mostly on my chest, but I think it can make subtle difference. I wake up with soft skin when I use this regularly. To illustrate how much I love this thing -- I am proud of them for making it to Sephora. 

Who knows, this could be your "one," too. It's definitely worth trying. No hate panning here.
P515486.Title: "The One"

P515486.ReviewText:
Not usually the type to write reviews but I cannot rave about this product enough. Excited to see it at Sephora! 

Will note that I do prefer the original formulation more. They reformulated some time last year, assuming part of it was so that they can produce and distribute globally. I don’t know much about what changed - pls do your own due diligence on that. 

I repurchase this product time and time again as this was what helped me heal my skin barrier through retinoids/tretinoin. 

It is thick and creamy without being too greasy. My combination skin drinks this up and it doesn’t feel like it’s just sitting on top of my skin. It sits really well under sunscreen and makeup and has not pilled on me (though I’m sure layers of different products add to that). 

Again, cannot rave about this product enough. Will not gatekeep this since we all deserve healthy skin barriers ☺️

P515486.Title: Gentle yet effective moisturizer

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: I have dry and dehydrated skin. I started tretinoin a while ago. At the time, I was using the Avene TriXera Nutrition balm until it got discontinued. I wanted to try the Aestura cream because it has ceramides and cholesterol similar to the Avene. My skin was peeling and burning as expected from tretinoin. When I used the Aestura cream, it calmed down the burning sensation and smoothed the peeling skin. A year later, my skin has adapted to tretinoin without any issues. I use tretinoin 3 times a week and I use the Aestura cream every night. I wake up with moisturized skin in the morning. I don't use this product in the morning because it's a bit heavy under my sunscreen. I use the Cosrx hyaluronic acid cream instead because it's super lightweight. The only issue I have with this product is that the spreadability is not that great. Because I have dry skin, I apply moisturizers when my face is still a little bit wet or damp, and I find that this product doesn't spread nicely on top of wet skin. Other than that, it's a fantastic product and I will continue to buy it. One bottle lasted me 5 months! It's pricey, but for me it's worth it.
P515486.Title: Perfect with tretinoin

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: I love korean beauty and im glad it's finally making it's way here to the US. This cream is exactly what i expected it to be and more. I live in a colder state where it's been negatives recently and my skin has been so dry. With this cream my skin has felt so hydrated and nourished. My skin loved it so much and it did not break me out. It also worked well with my other skincare and makeup

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: The ATOBARRIER365 Cream with Ceramides is hydrating and I love the visible ceramides. I was curious about the differences between this brand's products as they are all labelled for hydration/moisturization and the skin barrier. They all hydrate my dry skin. I believe the soothing cream is best for the skin barrier. The lotion has a similar texture when applying but maybe slightly lighter. The regular cream contains visible ceramide capsules that burst open when applied to the skin. I feel that only one or two of these products are necessary. I would apply the cream first, followed by the soothing cream, as it is thicker and helps to lock in the ceramides.
P515486.Title: visible ceramides

P515486.ReviewText: Aestura atobarrier cream is an interesting product. The packaging is very minimalistic but functional. The product itself comes out as a thick cream. Once you start to press it into the skin, it just melts into the skin effortlessly. I look forward to seeing long term effects from this product for those times where my barrier feels compromised by too many actives.

P515486.ReviewText: I have had the driest skin this season and couldn’t wait to give this product a try! I love how you can see the capsules of ceramides in the barrier cream. They blend seamlessly into the skin and leave my skin glowy and hydrated!

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: I have eczema and super dry skin. This is honestly one of my top 3 moisturizers. It is so good at sealing in the moisture into my skin. I notice that it pills ever so slightly however it is so worth it.
P515486.Title: So good??!!

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: Great cream to use with my hyaluronic acid serum. It helps to keep the skin hydrated for longer. The ingredients are safe, it doesn't have phenoxyethanol and it was exactly what I was looking for. I also found it to be good value for money, as you only need a small amount of the product for your entire face and neck.

P515486.OriginalProductName: Mini ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText:
Got a damaged skin barrier from tret or over-exfoliation? Wind burn? Sad sensitive skin in general? My friend, this is THE CREAM FOR YOU. 

This has been my holy grail barrier repair cream for years. Seriously desert-island, “if I could only own a single skincare product for the rest of my life” stuff. I’ve been purchasing it on Korean import sites, and I’m so excited that more people are getting easy access to it now. I plan to switch to Sephora when I run out of my current tube since the ingredients are identical.

I’ve got the sort of face that gets irritated if you look at it wrong: rosacea, hormonal acne, extreme fragrance sensitivities, apparent inability to hold onto moisture, the works. Nothing has been better for me than this at night and in the winter, with the corresponding Hydro cream (blue tube, a little lighter in texture) in the morning and during humid summer weather. 

If you’re like me, you’ll understand when I say this stuff is boring in the best way. It’s absolutely packed with soothing and moisturizing ingredients that have years of science behind them. It applies easily and absorbs surprisingly quickly for something so dense. It’s amazing. I love it. I hope you’ll love it too.
P515486.Title: The gold standard in barrier repair!

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: I absolutely love this cream. It’s my go to whenever I need an extra layer of protection and moisture. I highly recommend for all that get facial treatments during winter as this has helped my skin from purging.  It leaves my skin glowing!
P515486.Title: Love

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: Sephora carrying this brand is a blessing. I have always used this cream, it works so well. I use it with my tret and it does not give me flakes in the mornjng. If has ceramides in it which plump your skin and keep it hydrated. So easy to shop at sephora for it now.
P515486.Title: Dry skin savior

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: I put this on after applying tretinoin. It provides adequate hydration and barrier protection for my skin, so I don’t get any dry flakes or any other discomfort with tretinoin. It is super emollient so it can feel a bit greasy.
P515486.Title: Pairs well with tretinoin

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: i’m absolutely heartbroken that this formula changed and now contains pore-clogging ingredients. this moisturizer was my absolute favorite and i had no idea how i lived without it for so long…. now i will not be repurchasing again.
P515486.Title: no longer acne safe!!

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: Saw the reviews and had this on my list as a must-try. Super happy I did along with their other products! Huge improvement to dryness and flakiness on my forehead and cheeks after 1 week of use. The mist product has also been a great add on for daily on-the-go hydration. HAPPY!
P515486.Title: Saved my dry and flakey forehead!

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText:
I used to buy this abroad or shipped from Korea. The formula change of removing high grade silicones and waxes in exchange for Niacinamide just turns this to another ceramide moisturizer. Might as well buy CeraVa, Vanicream or Prequel you know? The little balls are just gimmicky now. Plus now it’s less occlusive. In plain English, it doesn’t lock in moisture and hydration as much anymore. 

And Niacinamide is an excellent ingredient for brightening (any dermatologist would agree), but it’s a no-no for the usual target market (former I guess?) of this product who have reallly realllly sensitive, irritated skin who wants BARRIER repair with no actives. Some might still be okay with the Niacinamide, but think post tretinoin, eczema or rosacea.
P515486.Title: Formula Changed, Less Occlusive

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: I have extremely sensitive skin prone to excema and breakouts. After a particularly bad allergic reaction to down feathers and horrible swelling for a week, my face was left wrinkled, the texture of a snake and patches of rxcema. My whole face burned especially my eyelids even after exposure to water, and even to my usual sensitive moisturizer. This helped my skin get back to normal in less than a week, and didn’t burn at all upon application.
P515486.Title: Amazing - esp when skin barrier has been damaged

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: I love this moisturizer and I’ve gone through 3 bottles so far. I have really sensitive, dry and eczema prone skin and this stuff is amazing. I highly recommend!

P515486.ReviewText: I've been using this Aestura Autobarrier 365 cream for over a year and absolutely love it. I have normal to dry mature skin and it is the best moisturizer on this planet. Blush on my cheeks and a lip gloss. This moisturizer keeps your skin plumped and moist for 48 hours. It is the #1 selling moisturizer by dermatologists in South Korea. I like a minimal daily skin care routine. I use the Anua Heartleaf deep pore cleansing foam - another fantastic product available on Amazon. My skin has never looked so plumped and juicy since I started using this moisturizer. 100x better than high end creams for such a reasonable price. Highly recommend. :-)

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText:
Disclaimers: 
- I never used the old version of this, and I only used this product twice. 
- My skin is very sensitive/reactive. 
- My normal moisturizer is Cetaphil moisturizing cream, and my comparisons use this as a control.

I heard good things about this moisturizer, and wanted to try something different, and of course I have been wanting to steer more towards Korean skincare as its gentler and often times more affordable. However, after two uses:
- I noticed that this cream is not as moisturizing as my Cetaphil cream, I could feel some tightness in my skin, even after applying another layer. 
- It didn't make me feel greasy or look oily, however, it did feel like it was getting into my eyes and making them feel filmy, if you know what I mean. 
- It didn't irritate my skin or make it sore/itchy, however I did get a few small whiteheads on my cheeks and forehead, and also developed a pimple under my nostril. I'm not super acne prone, so this was a red flag for me. 
- And for those who are sensitive to smells, it is unscented, but it does have that unscented scent which was also a little off for me.

Is it horrible? No. It will work well for many people. Did it work for me? Unfortunately, no.
P515486.Title: I really wanted to like this...

My foundation was always either slipping off and smearing on everything which I never have an issue with, or I was constantly powdering my face because it felt super greasy which made my makeup feel heavy. The product feels nice and goes on the skin lovely and if it didn't cause breakouts I would continue for night and days I don't wear make-up.  I do have super sensitive skin, and it didn't cause any irritation which is why I tried so hard to make it work.

However, I was disappointed when I opened it and noticed the difference immediately. The product on the left is the original, and on the right is the one I received from sephora. The texture of the original is more of a thick creamy consistency, and the one I received is more sticky and sunscreen consistency. It feels completely different on the face as well. I have sensitive skin, and it doesn’t feel like it moisturizes the same anymore. I’m not sure if they changed the formula, or rebranded but this is not the product I’ve been using. However, the more I used it, the more I realized of two things: 1) my skin started to feel more dehydrated than usual (I have oily dehydrated skin) and my eyes would get so irritated in the evening and in the morning they would burn. No cream has ever caused me this no matter how close I get to the eye area. This cream is greasy but I didn’t care because I used it at night, however, it wasn’t effective at all and because it is not hydrating, my barrier feels worse. It’s great for the hands though.

P515486.ReviewText: I’ve been using this for almost two months, and I wish I was more impressed. The texture is beautiful, but it takes so long to absorb into my skin. There’s no stickiness, but just feeling it sit on my face for what feels like 17 light years is very annoying. It’s a guessing game each time trying not to apply way too much or way too little. Also, I haven’t found it to be moisturizing. If anything, the texture of my skin has gotten worse. The cold weather where I live could be a contributing factor, but I’m still disappointed that such a nice feeling cream left my skin congested and dehydrated (and sometimes a little flaky). If you’re sensitive or acne-prone, I’d either give this a patch test or skip it unfortunately.

P515486.ReviewText: Used this after Tretinoin in the evening as recommended by a few forums, and while it did go on nicely at first, in the morning my face was very dry in certain spots, and reapplication caused pilling. I also noticed a few new breakouts with a few days use.
P515486.Title: Almost perfect - but drying

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText:
This moisturizer is deeply hydrating. My skin feels so plump and comfortable when I use it, especially at night. It gives that rich, cocooned feeling without actually clogging my pores or breaking me out.

The texture is interesting — it feels oily going on, but it’s not greasy in a heavy or pore-clogging way. It kind of melts into the skin and creates a protective barrier, which I love for nighttime.

For my dry girlies: I am super, super dry, and this is incredibly hydrating at night. I wish I could use it in the morning too because my skin drinks it up. But alas… I cannot. The slight oily slip makes it tricky under makeup and doesn’t sit the best under foundation, so it’s not my favorite for daytime wear.

Overall, it’s an amazing nighttime moisturizer for dry or barrier-compromised skin. Just not my first choice for daytime under makeup. 4/5!
P515486.Title: Great night time moisturizer!

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: I only recently learned that a true barrier cream includes three specific ingredients. This has totally saved my skin this winter. It’s so soothing and protective. Will definitely repurchase.
P515486.Title: A new must-have

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: As someone who did not grow up in cold weather, my skin has been crying for hydration this winter. All the cracking and dryness decreased after a few uses, and there is no fragrance (so I don't have to worry about my allergies).

P515486.ReviewText: I recently tried the ATOBARRIER365 Cream for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair after receiving it in exchange for my honest review. I have to say, I was pleasantly surprised by its effectiveness. The cream has a nice, lightweight texture that absorbs quickly into my skin without any greasy residue. After a few days of use, I noticed my skin felt more hydrated and resilient, especially in the areas that had been dry and irritated. I appreciate that it includes ingredients geared toward repairing the skin barrier, and I found it soothing on my sensitive skin. The scent is mild and pleasant, which is a bonus for me as I tend to prefer fragrance-free products. Overall, I think ATOBARRIER365 is a solid option for anyone looking to improve their skin’s moisture barrier. Just keep in mind that individual results may vary, and it’s always best to patch-test new products.

P515486.OriginalProductName: ATOBARRIER365 Cream Moisturizer with Ceramides & Niacinamide for Skin Moisture Barrier Repair
P515486.ReviewText: This barrier cream has been a lifesaver for my insanely dry winter skin. At night, I slather it on as the last step in my routine, and by morning, my skin still feels soft and hydrated. In the morning, I use a light layer before applying my tinted serum, and it keeps my makeup locked in all day without any dry patches or flaking. The texture is rich but not heavy, and it actually feels like it’s sealing in moisture instead of just sitting on top of my skin. Between this and drinking tons of water, my skin is finally coming back to life after months of being dull and tight. The ceramides and lipids really make a difference—I don’t get that midday dehydration where my skin feels like it’s pulling. I also love that it’s dermatologist-tested and designed to strengthen the skin barrier over time. It’s not just a quick fix; it actually improves dryness and flakiness with continued use. If your skin gets painfully dry in the winter like mine, this is worth trying.

Perfect for dry, sensitive, or even compromised skin, it provides an immediate soothing effect while helping to restore the skin’s lipid barrier over time. The texture is silky, absorbing quickly without leaving any greasy residue, which makes it a great choice for both daytime and nighttime use. After a few days of consistent use, you’ll notice your skin feels more supple and resilient, with a noticeable reduction in dryness or irritation. It’s especially beneficial for people with conditions like eczema or those living in harsh climates where the skin is constantly exposed to environmental stressors. The fragrance-free formula is a bonus for sensitive skin, and it’s suitable for all skin types, even those prone to allergies. Pros: • Deeply hydrates and nourishes skin • Contains ceramides to restore the skin barrier • Lightweight, fast-absorbing formula • Fragrance-free, suitable for sensitive skin • Great for dry or irritated skin, including eczema Cons: • May not be rich enough for very dry skin types in extreme conditions • On the pricier side for a drugstore moisturizer Overall, Atobarrier 365 is a reliable, gentle cream that lives up to its claims of providing long-lasting moisture and skin barrier repair. It’s a solid choice for daily use, especially if you need something that works without causing irritation.
